data.raw_description:
Standard Time is excited to welcome Jump Source into our space with a very special open to close set!

Jump Source – composed of Priori and Patrick Holland – venture through various strains of dance music in the studio, focusing on intuitive long-form recordings full of quirks. Though their independent work differs, the through-line of influence can be heard across the JS catalogue, and through their choice of records in the club.

Join us for a very special evening!
data.address: 165 Geary Avenue, Toronto, Ontario M6H 2B8, Canada
data.name: Jump Source - Open to Close

data.raw_description:
Rich Medina and Skratch Bastid at Standard Time!

Rich Medina is an elite DJ who’s captivating narrative plays like no other. His love affair with music began in his family’s church, arguably from the womb, and has blossomed into an internationally renowned career as a platinum selling record producer, recording artist, poet, journalist, curator, university and arts institution educator, and 2021 Pew Fellowship recipient.

Medina is sonic storyteller par excellence. His legendary Lil Ricky’s Rib Shack party shook APT’s dancefloor to its core for nine years in Manhattan’s Meatpacking District. Via Jump-N-Funk, North America’s original Fela Kuti tribute, Rich provided a blueprint for the world’s newfound interest in Afrobeat, which stands without peer to this day. His HOME residencies in NYC, Philadelphia and Los Angeles pay tribute to Larry Levan and the foundations of classic New York club life. In 2021, Rich was selected as the primary artist-in-residence and Music Director of Miami’s first Japanese-inspired listening room, Dante’s HiFi+.

As a highly respected spoken word artist, Rich has performed on stages around the world, and his sonorous voice has been utilized by Fisker, EA Sports, Nike and Pioneer. As an educator, Medina has lectured at TEDxPhilly and at his alma mater Cornell University, amongst numerous others. As a music producer, he’s collaborated with a range of artists, including Jill Scott, J Dilla, Bobbito Garcia and Phil Asher.

Rich Medina is the modern-day renaissance man—an artist and visionary whose journey continues, expands and inspires.

Skratch Bastid has been moving dance floors and captivating crowds across the globe for over two decades, establishing himself as one of the world’s most respected and beloved DJs. From packed clubs and massive stages to his legendary annual Bastid’s BBQs, he brings the same energy and precision to every stage he touches. Known for his electric stage presence, technical prowess and versatile selections that span the complete musical spectrum—from hip hop to house, disco to dancehall, reggae to funk and back again—Bastid is a true crowd favourite with an unrivaled ability to create unforgettable moments with not only music, but the community.

While touring was on hold over the lockdowns, Bastid added live-streaming to his performance repertoire with his weekly ‘Tuesday Morning Coffee’ stream which has allowed him to connect directly with his supporters through his own community membership called ‘Top Grillin’ VIP”. Thanks to his high-energy sets, charismatic personality and unbound musical knowledge, he has quickly become one of the most watched and followed DJs on Twitch.

From humble beginnings in Bedford, Nova Scotia, Bastid cut his teeth in the battle scene of the late 90s and early 2000s by competing in the DMCs, International Turtablist Federation and Scribble Jam. The latter produced one of his most iconic moments, the “Imperial March” routine that became his first brush with viral fame, and has over 10 million views on YouTube alone (and countless others from before social media even officially existed).

Bastid proved early on that he offered much more than just impressive tricks from his viral fame.

He’s also an accomplished producer with multiple Juno nominations, known for his collaborations with artists like Shad and DJ Jazzy Jeff. His demanding touring schedule has brought him all over the world, sometimes playing up to 200 shows a year in over 50 countries and in 2024, he had the honour of performing at Red Rocks Amphitheatre.

Bastid continues to expand his empire into many other successful creative ventures including the infamous Bastid’s BBQ, his very own traveling party in select global cities that plays host to a who’s who of hip hop and DJ culture including appearances by DJ Premier, 9th Wonder, Maseo, Brother Ali, A-Trak, Pete Rock, Lord Finesse and many, many more.

Most recently, Bastid has launched a curator page on Apple Music under Bastid’s BBQ to share spatial audio recordings and DJ mixes from the extremely talented pool of DJs from all over the world who come to party and perform at Bastid’s BBQ!
data.address: 165 Geary Avenue, Toronto, Ontario M6H 2B8, Canada
data.name: Rich Medina and Skratch Bastid

data.raw_description:
Buy tickets

̸

Lineup

Daniel Avery

Vjollca

Share

Standard Time welcomes the return of Daniel Avery alongside Vjollça!

At what point does a record end and an altered state of mind begin? Where does all that caustic noise and ecstatic melody that’s been pressed in a record’s grooves begin to cognitively shift your perceptions and help lift you out of the mundane into a space to inhabit, an escape from the world outside?

Following all that sound will bring you to Tremor – the breathtaking sixth album by Daniel Avery. An inner world built from chaotic noise, transcendental ambience and voices that echo around it like pulsing transmissions from some distant ether world. All those sounds on Tremor sublimely flow and blur together to leave the listener disconnected from the here and now. It’s as close to magic as music can get.

“I’ve never had a more inspiring time making a record” says Avery. “This is a living and breathing collective. Since the earliest recordings, Tremor felt like a studio in the sky, a space in time through we could all pass. Every person on the record leaves their unique mark but the power lies in us all working together. It’s a beautiful lesson acid house has taught me: no one’s energy is more important than anyone else’s… especially my own.”

Tremor opens the door to The Kills’ Alison Mosshart (“Her voice signifies an exciting time in my life when I first started heading out into the night as a teenager… she’s cool as fuck and completely genuine”), Walter Schreifels from Quicksand / Rival Schools (“Those bands were a massive part of my youth, he brings such a clarity with his voice”) as well as a slew of contemporaries in bdrmm, Julie Dawson from NewDad, yeule, Ellie, Art School Girlfriend, yuné pinku, and Cecile Believe.

All of the energy poured into Tremor by Avery and the collective he built has created a free- flowing album that effortlessly draws multiple different styles of music into one shimmering whole. Rhythm tracks shudder and quake like techno records or jungle breaks transmitted from the bottom of the ocean – each a huge sonorous, slowed down depth charge blasting out of the planet’s inner core. An acid drone on Haze flows in on a thunderous sub-bass line that carries the kind of power that could reshape landscapes around it, while the ethereal ambience shared by album opener Neon Pulse and A Memory Wrapped In Paper And Smoke floats on with the elegance of summer rain falling onto still glass.

Elsewhere, the album’s title track blurs a series of scratched records with a guitar line that sounds like it’s just split the heavens apart in a series of notes that perfectly connects this world and the stars above us and a slow motion 4/4 kick drum drives forward a track that feels like a euphoric anthem for the end of the world (I Feel You). The first release from the album – Rapture In Blue – flows in on a slow-motion breakbeat that helps lift LA-based singer Cecile Believe’s beguiling voice to a truly celestial plane. Heard together, the music is completely transportational.

“With previous releases, I’ve talked about the cleansing power of pure distortion, the broken beauty of noise and the enchantment of melodies hidden under multiple waves of sound. Now it feels like those ideas are being transmitted in Technicolor. My life has changed dramatically in the twelve years since Drone Logic was released. We’ve all lost people to the universe and we’ve learnt so much – good and bad. Life has changed in every way. All of that experience is in this record, it has totally shaped it. It’s a hugely expansive version of what I’ve done before. This is a record for the post-rave comedown kids, the guitar heads and anyone else who wants to come along for the ride. Everyone is welcome.”

Tremor arrives at a point where Avery’s workload must appear colossal to the outsider. A producer who DJs, a musician who remixes and an artist continually collaborating, he has recently reconstructed The Cure’s DRONE:NODRONE to universal love and his work as one third of Demise of Love (a group formed with Ghost Culture and Working Men’s Club) currently soundtracks the hazy, opaque edges of dancefloors around the world. The album itself is Avery’s first release for Domino – arguably the world’s most brilliantly free-thinking independent record label.

“If I was to write a list of people who have shaped my life, Erol \[Alkan\] would be at the very top. I am so grateful for my time with his label Phantasy and all the work we’ve done together over the years. When Domino came along, it was a natural step. I remember playing tracks to them and saying, ‘Imagine if we could get Alan Moulder to mix this.’ And we got him. He has mixed some of my favourite records of all time – Smashing Pumpkins, Nine Inch Nails, A Perfect Circle. He’s someone who creates an incredible warmth in music when it’s at its heaviest. David Wrench – another magician – mixed the other half of the record, bringing such a delicate, pure and intricate touch. Alongside \[mastering engineer\] Heba Kadry, they are pillars of the Tremor collective.”

And at the heart of that collective, moving between each of those people is the architect of it all, the man who built the dimly lit, eternally-inviting space that comes alive each time the record played. “The record is me trying to find or create a place of inner peace… some people might say that’s a search for a higher power, others say it’s the feeling of getting lost in the pulse of the strobe light… it’s all the same thing. That’s what I’ve been searching for these last few years and it’s the space that Tremor is broadcasting from.”

Tune in, turn it up, embrace the tremors and prepare to leave this place behind.

Plus the one and only Vjollça will be setting the tone for the evening.

Do not miss - this will sell out!
data.address: 165 Geary Avenue, Toronto, Ontario M6H 2B8, Canada
data.name: Daniel Avery and Vjollça

Do not miss - this will sell out!

Presented by Standard Time.

This is a 19+ event.

data.raw_description:
Standard Time is happy to welcome back Keys N Krates and friends for another Odd Soul party!

“Odd Soul” is a global dance music party curated by the members of Keys N Krates. They hand pick artists to create a lineup that brings good music and good vibes to an inclusive group of people who want to dance and party all night long.

Known for their idiosyncratic and organic sample-driven approach to dance music, Keys N Krates continues to evolve as artists and producers. The Toronto-based trio, made up of Adam Tune, David Matisse, and Greg Dawson, began as a live act that would flip popular hip-hop and dance music tunes. The trio then decided they wanted to create their own music and found success with their breakout single Dum Dee Dum, a song that became an anthem of the subgenre of instrumental trap music. Their affinity for flipping off-kilter samples and writing fun dance floor songs didn’t stop there. They continued to evolve their style over the years, injecting more of their childhood influences into their music until finally arriving at making house music from their perspective, which is influenced by hip hop, tribal rhythms, 90’s house music, RnB, and 70’s disco and soul.

Mix Signals is a Toronto-based DJ duo known for bold, high-energy performances and eclectic electronic sets built around hypnotic grooves and emotional peaks. Since debuting in December 2023, they’ve built a name in the underground scene with a residency at Insomnia, performances in Montreal, and openings for artists like M.I.A. KTK and Keys N Krates. From clubs to warehouse raves, Mix Signals creates unforgettable moments that last long after the music stops.

From the heart of Tokyo's electrifying nightlife to the thriving music scene of Toronto, Sakiko Nagai is redefining the dance floor with her irresistibly groovy sound. Specializing in disco, boogie, and house, she crafts sets that are a celebration of rhythm and funk. Her residency at 915 Dupont has been local’s favourite showcasing the best of city pop and boogie hits on vinyl.

Event admin

StandardTime

Update this event
data.address: 165 Geary Avenue, Toronto, Ontario M6H 2B8, Canada
data.name: Odd Soul featuring Keys N Krates, Mix Signals and Sakiko Nagai

data.raw_description:
Standard Time and NUNU come together to present NTS royalty and international house music DJ diva Moxie for a night of wild dancing that will leave breathless. Toronto’s own Kiki LeFreak, Karim Olen Ash and Phillippe will be rounding out this ultra special night.

Moxie is a dance music powerhouse who broadcasts her iconic voice as one of London’s most in-demand DJs and radio hosts, continuing to be a constant fixture of clubs and festivals globally.

NTS Radio is Moxie’s weekly home on Wednesdays. For 13 years now she has maintained one of the longest standing residencies on the internet radio behemoth, where her iconic voice accompanies the very best in deep house, Detroit techno and just about anything dubby, groovy and percussive that’s bursting with soul. Moxie often invites guests, with past features including Jeff Mills, Steve Spacek, DJ Holographic, Four Tet, Or:la & more.

When not hosting radio, Moxie tours globally as one of the circuits much loved DJs. She touches down regularly in Panorama Bar, Glastonbury, Rainbow Disco Club, Bassiani, KOKO, Le Sucre,Love International, Houghton, Draaimolen, De School and Sonar BCN and has produced celebrated mixes for Resident Advisor, BBC 1’s Essential Mix, Beats In Space, Truants, FACT, Solid Steel, Triple J and more.

Further to this is Moxie’s renowned work as a party coordinator, label owner and voiceover artist. She founded On Loop in 2016, which as a label has released beloved music from producers across the globe such as Shanti Celeste, Desert Sound Colony, Matisa, Nicola Cruz and Yu Su while as a party hosted packed events in most clubbing institutions across Britain and Europe. Then there is Moxie's celebrated voiceover work, most noted in her commentary for Resident Advisor’s Narrated Shorts Film series covering dance music legends Drexciya, Burial and Jai Paul while also on their What Happened To Tech House?’ mini-documentary.

All of the above illustrates Moxie’s multidisciplinary practice in dance music, best highlighted by her tireless work ethic in her own career, while simultaneously uplifting artists and DJs in her orbit at any chance she gets
data.address: 165 Geary Avenue, Toronto, Ontario M6H 2B8, Canada
data.name: Moxie, Kiki LeFreak, Karim Olen Ash and Phillippe presented by NUNU x Standard Time

data.raw_description:
SHA Presents: Jackfruit at Standard Time!

With support from Hangaëlle

Accolade after accolade, TSHA has worked her way to the top echelons of UK dance music with scores of multi-country residencies, in-demand parties, and hotly-tipped releases under her belt. Now, she adds to those triumphs with the launch of her new record label, Jackfruit, after years spent nurturing and evolving her acclaimed event series of the same name.

As a DJ, producer, multi-instrumentalist and songwriter, Fareham-born artist Teisha has quietly become one of the UK’s most sought-after acts under her TSHA alias. Raised on a musical palette of classic house and garage thanks to her brother’s extensive record collection, TSHA’s distinguishable sound now arrives at the intersection of melodic house, Balearic, and high-tempo club music.

TSHA’s self-taught dexterity behind the decks has earned her shows from Glastonbury to Coachella and beyond, while holding down gigs at some of the UK’s most beloved clubs, including a recent four-week stint at London’s Phonox. With her club night and event series Jackfruit, TSHA puts the audience front and centre with a party made for “living in the moment”, with a no-phone policy that calls for pure connection and euphoria on the dancefloor. To date, Jackfruit has made stops everywhere from Pikes Ibiza to Movement Festival in Detroit.

TSHA’s Jackfruit party expands beyond its club form and becomes a record label home to ‘Revolution’, a recent single that muses on “the digital overwhelm” and breaking away from online noise. “‘Revolution’ is a play on Gil Scott-Heron’s ‘The Revolution Will Not Be Televised’, but this track is about putting your phone away and living in the moment,” she says. “As a party, Jackfruit is a place for escapism. It puts the punter at the centre rather than the DJ, and allows you to really lose yourself.”

Hangaëlle is a Toronto-based Mozambican DJ and producer. Known for her versatility, she is especially sought out for her genre-fluid sets that seamlessly weave Afro-electronic music, club hits, and global dance sounds into euphoric sonic experiences.

Having co-founded Kuruza and Amaproblem, and more recently moving into independent curatorial work with the launch of her first solo event, Mhmmm, at CODA Nightclub, her contributions to Toronto’s music landscape have continued to establish her as a key figure in the city’s club culture.

See you on May 15th - you don't want to miss this one!
data.address: 165 Geary Avenue, Toronto, Ontario M6H 2B8, Canada
data.name: TSHA presents: Jackfruit with special guest Hangaëlle
data.description:
SHA Presents: Jackfruit at Standard Time!

With support from Hangaëlle

Accolade after accolade, TSHA has worked her way to the top echelons of UK dance music with scores of multi-country residencies, in-demand parties, and hotly-tipped releases under her belt. Now, she adds to those triumphs with the launch of her new record label, Jackfruit, after years spent nurturing and evolving her acclaimed event series of the same name.

As a DJ, producer, multi-instrumentalist and songwriter, Fareham-born artist Teisha has quietly become one of the UK’s most sought-after acts under her TSHA alias. Raised on a musical palette of classic house and garage thanks to her brother’s extensive record collection, TSHA’s distinguishable sound now arrives at the intersection of melodic house, Balearic, and high-tempo club music.

TSHA’s self-taught dexterity behind the decks has earned her shows from Glastonbury to Coachella and beyond, while holding down gigs at some of the UK’s most beloved clubs, including a recent four-week stint at London’s Phonox. With her club night and event series Jackfruit, TSHA puts the audience front and centre with a party made for “living in the moment”, with a no-phone policy that calls for pure connection and euphoria on the dancefloor. To date, Jackfruit has made stops everywhere from Pikes Ibiza to Movement Festival in Detroit.

TSHA’s Jackfruit party expands beyond its club form and becomes a record label home to ‘Revolution’, a recent single that muses on “the digital overwhelm” and breaking away from online noise. “‘Revolution’ is a play on Gil Scott-Heron’s ‘The Revolution Will Not Be Televised’, but this track is about putting your phone away and living in the moment,” she says. “As a party, Jackfruit is a place for escapism. It puts the punter at the centre rather than the DJ, and allows you to really lose yourself.”

Hangaëlle is a Toronto-based Mozambican DJ and producer. Known for her versatility, she is especially sought out for her genre-fluid sets that seamlessly weave Afro-electronic music, club hits, and global dance sounds into euphoric sonic experiences.

Having co-founded Kuruza and Amaproblem, and more recently moving into independent curatorial work with the launch of her first solo event, Mhmmm, at CODA Nightclub, her contributions to Toronto’s music landscape have continued to establish her as a key figure in the city’s club culture.

See you on May 15th - you don't want to miss this one!

Presented by Standard Time.

This is an 19+ event
